channel.title: Open Floor Notes
channel.description: Quarterly choreography write-ups and class notes from Michelle Kemp, Hartford.

item.title: Why I restarted this blog
item.description: Twelve years of teaching and a notebook full of eight-counts I keep losing. So here it is, in public, one write-up a quarter. The work is showing up, and writing it down is part of showing up.

item.title: A winter warm-up that actually warms you up
item.description: Cold floors are hard on knees and on the right foot I keep babying. A joint-care sequence for the first ten minutes, low impact, before anyone touches a grapevine.

item.title: Showcase recap, and what "Open Window" taught the advanced team
item.description: The best fall showcase we have put on. The lift sequence finally landed, the solo spotlight held the room, and "Open Window" is now part of our repertoire. Thank you to every family that filled those seats.

item.title: Winter intensive: three Saturdays, one piece
item.description: December 5, 12, and 19. We clean up the showcase material and start building toward the new year. Bring layers and the indoor shoes, the floor runs cold this time of year.

item.title: What I am carrying into the new year
item.description: Eighteen months of slow is still eighteen months. The studio fund keeps ticking up, the plan keeps tightening, and the floor is still the best place I know to put a hard week down.
